issues.title: spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-chat-memory-* modules are missing from BOM
issues.body:
**Expected Behavior**

These newer modules should be added to `spring-ai-bom`. I see no reason not to include them.
* `spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-chat-memory-cassandra`
* `spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-chat-memory-jdbc`
* `spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-chat-memory-neo4j`


**Current Behavior**

Only `spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-chat-memory` is present in `spring-ai-bom`.
So for the other 3 submodules, dependents must manually pin the dependency version.

**Context**

The [1.0.0-M7 spring-ai-bom](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/v1.0.0-M7/spring-ai-bom/pom.xml#L428) that was just released this week.

issues.title: Spring-AI 1.0.0-M7 has been released while Ollama spring boot starter hasn't been updated to 1.0.0-M7 yet
issues.body:
**Expected Behavior**

The `spring-ai-ollama-spring-boot-starter` should be available in version `1.0.0-M7` to align with the latest Spring AI release.

**Current Behavior**

Spring AI is at version `1.0.0-M7` (https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/releases/tag/v1.0.0-M7), but the latest `spring-ai-ollama-spring-boot-starter` is still `1.0.0-M6` (https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.springframework.ai/spring-ai-ollama-spring-boot-starter/1.0.0-M6). This creates a version mismatch when upgrading.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fa31b180-e8e3-4c6c-8027-3515a64b8991)

**Context**

This version mismatch prevents smooth upgrades and requires manual dependency overrides. A matching `M7` release for the starter would resolve this.

comments.body:
spring-ai-ollama-spring-boot-starter has been renamed spring-ai-starter-model-ollama, which is present on [maven central here](https://central.sonatype.com/artifact/org.springframework.ai/spring-ai-starter-model-ollama/versions).

This is mentioned in the release notes here:
https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/v1.0.0-M7/spring-ai-docs/src/main/antora/modules/ROOT/pages/upgrade-notes.adoc#common-changes-across-versions
Thank you for sharing this documentation. It’s incredibly helpful.

issues.title: Support "reasoning_effort" option in AzureOpenAiChatOptions
issues.body:
**Current Behavior**

Currently, it is not possible to set the `reasoningEffort` parameter when using `AzureOpenAiChatOptions`. This functionality is only available for `OpenAiChatOptions`.

Working example with OpenAiChatModel:
```java
OpenAiChatOptions.builder()
                .reasoningEffort("low")
                .build();
```



Non-functional example with AzureOpenAiChatModel:
```java
AzureOpenAiChatOptions.builder()
                .reasoningEffort("low")
                .build();
```



**Expected Behavior**

`AzureOpenAiChatOptions` should support the `reasoningEffort` parameter in the same way that `OpenAiChatOptions` does. This would allow users to configure the reasoning effort level when using Azure OpenAI models.

**Context**

Adding this functionality would enhance spring ai flexibility, enabling Azure enterprise users to utilize OpenAI o1 and o3 models with custom reasoning effort configurations. This is particularly valuable for organizations relying on Azure.


**Version:** 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT

comments.body:
### OpenAI
The `reasoning_effort` parameter is available.
- Documentation: [OpenAI API Reference](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create#chat-create-reasoning_effort)

### Azure OpenAI
The `reasoning_effort` parameter is not yet available in the General Availability (GA) API but is included in the Preview API.
- GA Documentation: [Azure OpenAI GA Reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/reference)
- Preview Documentation: [Azure OpenAI Preview Reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/reference-preview)

@dev-jonghoonpark Thank you for your explanation.

I am new to the community and still learning how things work here. Could you please clarify your response? Does this mean that we should only implement this feature for AzureOpenAiChatOptions once it becomes available in the GA API?

Hello @andresssantos 

I’ve just organized the relevant information.

As far as I know, there is currently no way to enable the Azure OpenAI model's preview API in Spring AI.
If there is, we can implement it, but if not, that functionality needs to be implemented first.

The Azure model support delegates to this class in the Azure SDK

https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-java/blob/main/sdk/openai/azure-ai-openai/src/main/java/com/azure/ai/openai/models/ChatCompletionsOptions.java

We can't add that option as I don't see how we can map it onto the underlying library.  

Also to note for added confusion, this sdk is eventually going to be deprecated as Microsoft is moving to use the java sdk that openai provides, adding their own security layer on top. 

Perhaps you can use the openai client, not the microsoft one, as it has a reasoning effort property.
@mkheck is my analysis of the lack of support for this in the Azure SDK correct.?
@markpollack I have this queued up to research. More soon.

@markpollack @mkheck Opened PR #3048 to check if `reasoning_effort` can be supported—tested locally and it works.  

If I’ve misunderstood, feel free to close the PR.
That works. I had just begun to dig into it when I saw the notification it was merged. 😬
